title: Project Report: E-Commerce Order Microservice — Java Backend Developer
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Order service with inventory checks, JWT auth, Redis cache, and Kafka event publishing.
objectives: Add Redis caching for product catalog
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Intermediate)*

**E-Commerce Order Microservice** is a intermediate-level end-to-end project for Java Backend Developer. Duration: 4–5 weeks. Order service with inventory checks, JWT auth, Redis cache, and Kafka event publishing. Tools: Spring Boot 3, Kafka, Redis, PostgreSQL.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Implement JWT authentication
Objective: Publish OrderCreated events to Kafka
Objective: Add Redis caching for product catalog
Objective: Integration tests with Testcontainers
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Java Backend Developer portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Java Backend Developer resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Java Backend Developer resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Domain
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Domain** *(Level: Intermediate)*

Order and OrderItem entities with state machine. Complete these tasks: Order statuses; Inventory client. Deliverable for this phase: Domain model docs.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Order statuses
- [ ] Inventory client
```

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Intermediate)*

Submit all final deliverables: Architecture diagram; Running microservice; Grafana dashboard; Postman collection. Skills demonstrated: Microservices, Kafka, Security, Caching. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: Architecture diagram
Deliverable: Running microservice
Deliverable: Grafana dashboard
Deliverable: Postman collection
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits Architecture diagram and passes the rubric.
